assertions.reason: The targeted open-thread widget flow passed while opening thread-123 from the list and asserting that the detail header, title, and thread ID matched the selected row.

The same widget flow passed after asserting distinct User prompt, Assistant output, Plan update, Terminal output, and File change sections with representative command and file-change content, showing visually differentiated item types.

The live-stream widget flow passed after injecting a message delta into the open thread and asserting the streamed assistant output became visible without reopening or manually refreshing the detail view.
The mixed-item widget flow passed while plan, command, and file-change events rendered in their own labeled visual sections instead of collapsing into generic text.

The lifecycle widget flow passed after a threadStatusChanged event updated the open detail view to Completed and the list row also showed Completed after navigating back, confirming synchronized lifecycle state updates.
The timeline-history widget flow passed after loading earlier history and asserting the newly revealed older entries appeared ahead of later entries in coherent chronological order.
The thread-switch widget flow passed while updates from the off-screen thread remained hidden in the currently selected detail view and the newly selected thread showed only its own live deltas.
The unavailable-detail widget flow passed by first showing a clear Thread unavailable fallback with a Retry affordance, then successfully recovering to the expected detail content after retry.

frictions: The flutter-mobile-widget-tests surface provides assertion-backed Flutter logs rather than standalone screenshots, videos, or websocket captures, so this report relies on saved command logs that encode the visible widget expectations exercised in each passing flow.
